name: ts_check
description: Run TypeScript compiler type checking (tsc --noEmit) on a project. Shows type errors with file locations, supports filtering by pattern, and limits the number of errors displayed to avoid overwhelming output. Replaces 'cd /app && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1' with a single script call that includes filtering and summary.
when_to_use: Use when you need to verify TypeScript types are correct after making code changes. Replaces manual 'cd <project> && npx tsc --noEmit' commands. Use --grep to filter errors related to your changes (e.g., --grep=lazy to see only errors in lazy-related files). Use --max to limit output and focus on the most relevant errors first.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the TypeScript project (e.g., /app)

parameters.name: --grep
parameters.description: Show only errors matching this pattern (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: --max
parameters.description: Maximum number of errors to show (default: 50)

parameters.name: --tsconfig
parameters.description: Path to tsconfig.json relative to project root (default: tsconfig.json)

parameters.name: --no-cd
parameters.description: Stay in current directory instead of cd'ing to project_root

examples.expected: Runs tsc --noEmit and shows up to 50 type errors.
Shows up to 10 type errors mentioning 'window'.
Shows all type errors without limit.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate steps (cd to project, run tsc, grep/filter output for relevant errors) with a single script call. Built-in --grep filtering eliminates a separate grep step when debugging specific features. The --max limit prevents token overflow from hundreds of type errors. Eliminates the error-prone manual parsing of tsc output format.
